summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of the Search Typeahead HLD app. 13/13 backend pytest tests pass covering health, suggest (prefix/empty/non-existent), cache hit on repeat with consistent hashing nodeId stability, search submission, trending visibility, trending mode with score field, batch writes + admin/flush, consistent hash debug (ringSize=192, virtualNodes=64, sample=48 entries), admin clear-cache & reset-metrics, and metrics shape (cache nodes A/B/C, latency p50/p95, batch writesIssued/writesSaved, trie.keys=124816). Frontend Playwright run confirmed homepage loads, debounced suggestions populate (10 items for 'iph'), cache-strip badge shows 'Cache MISS · cache-node-B', Enter submits a search and the query appears in the Trending Now panel within ~2.5s, mode-basic/mode-trending tabs switch, CacheDebugPanel buttons (btn-force-flush, btn-clear-cache, btn-reset-metrics) work, HashRingVisualizer SVG renders with 50 circles, ring-probe-input updates ring-debug-result with owner node, and /architecture renders all section headings (9 sections including bonus 'Suggestion request flow').

integration_issues.issue: Switching to mode-trending while typing 'java' returned 0 suggestions in the UI even though backend /api/suggest?q=java&mode=trending returns items with 'score'. Likely the SearchBox is not re-issuing the suggest call when the mode tab changes, or it filters by a score threshold. Backend works; UI re-fetch on mode change is the suspect.

action_items: Optional: ensure SearchBox re-fires /api/suggest when the mode tab changes (currently mode change may not trigger a re-fetch for the current prefix, so trending suggestions render empty until the user retypes).
critical_code_review_comments: Trending mode UI is not re-fetching suggestions on mode switch — investigate useEffect dependency on `mode` in SearchBox.jsx.

Trending panel still contains stale test data ('batchtest alpha/beta/gamma', 'iphone 17 pro max') — consider exposing an admin endpoint to reset trending or rely on natural time-decay; for demos a hard reset is convenient.
Metrics latency keys are p50LatencyMs/p95LatencyMs while the spec called them p50/p95 — pick one convention and document it in /architecture#api-reference.
Backend gateway /api/health on FastAPI proxy responded fine, but consider adding a /api/version that surfaces Node child PID for ops visibility.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (1 minor integration gap on mode-tab re-fetch)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test created queries: 'iphone 17 pro max', 'java programming', 'batchtest alpha/beta/gamma/delta', 'apple', 'test'. They persisted into the trending panel; recommend a trending-reset admin endpoint for cleanliness.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend gateway is FastAPI:8001 → Node:8002. /api/* prefix is enforced. Trie keys ~124k after bootstrap. Latency metrics use p50LatencyMs/p95LatencyMs (not p50/p95). Ring sample length is 48 (>=24 required). cache.nodeId returns null for empty prefix (correct). Mode tab re-fetch on the frontend may be missing — quick check is to type 'java', then click mode-trending, then observe if a /api/suggest?...&mode=trending request fires.
rca of the issue: Trending mode 0-result: when user clicks the mode-trending tab while a prefix is already typed, SearchBox likely does not include `mode` in its useEffect dependency list, so it doesn't refire the suggest call. Mitigation: add `mode` to the dependency array in the suggest-fetching effect in /app/frontend/src/components/SearchBox.jsx (or wherever the suggest call lives), and ensure the call URL includes mode=trending.
